purpose: A compact judge-facing map from award criteria to public evidence, built so a rushed reviewer can verify the entry without accounts, APIs, private data, or hidden services.

recommended_review_order.why: Confirms the submission is a playable timed rotor puzzle before any write-up or proof layer.
Runs the same static page through the full four-phase route and produces the stable receipt.
Recomputes the sample receipt fields locally: four phases, 2907 score, 62 shifts, checksum Y5VFX1.
Maps theme fit, Turing fit, playability proof, media, manifest, and source links in one page.

rubric_map.claim: The solstice prompt is mechanical: daylight is the shared timer, nightfall is the failure state, and the completed receipt closes the longest-day run.
The build turns solstice and Turing into a playable rotor-alignment loop rather than a trivia page, static calendar, or proof-only wrapper.
Static HTML/CSS/JS supports touch, keyboard, auto demo, low-motion review, no-storage review, Nightfall recovery, smoke testing, and receipt verification.
The DEV article, README, judge page, manifest, and first-minute card give a short review path without asking judges to infer category fit from source.
The ode is carried by rotor-like state, XOR/BIN glyph language, target checking, checksum reasoning, and transparent local verification.

public_safety_boundary.browser_storage: Only a numeric local best-score key is used by default; ?nostore=1 disables best-score reads and writes.

stable_receipt.boundary: Transparent demo-run evidence only; not identity, eligibility, tax, payout, or anti-cheat proof.

human_gates: Push the local package with user approval.
Enable GitHub Pages and confirm public HTTP 200 links.
Run public-preflight.ps1 -Public after Pages is live.
Publish the DEV article only after public links are live and the logged-in challenge flow accepts the entry.
